content:
Is Thunderbird desktop connecting to your email provider using the Pop3, rather than Imap protocol?

Pop3 downloads and stores emails locally on your desktop computer. Once downloaded, emails are typically deleted from the server.

The Android app can only download emails on your providers server. Older emails stored on your computer would not be available.

https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/computer-networks/differences-between-pop3-and-imap/
creator: platform34

content:
Hi Ray

Directly creating folders within the app is not currently supported.

To add a new folder, login to your web mail and create the imap folder or label. The new folder will then be available within Thunderbird for Android after refreshing it's folder list.

To refresh the apps folder list.

Open the side bar with your account folders displayed (Inbox etc). Tap manage folders (bottom of list). Tap the three dot menu > refresh folder list.

The community have been voting on Mozilla connect for the feature to create imap folders within the app. You can show support by up-voting the idea below.

https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/add-the-ability-to-create-imap-folders/idi-p/72777
creator: platform34

content:
Hi @jesus

Could you clarify if automatic sync does, or does not work? Your statements contradict.

  • "app does sync automatically"
  • "I must open app an manually sync"

The most common cause of sync not working correctly is because the app has not been granted permission to run in the background.

To check,

  1. Open Android settings > apps > Thunderbird for Android > battery usage:
  2. Toggle "Allow background activity" ON.
